2003-05-01.title: British Muslims Are Seen Moving Into Mideast Terrorism
2003-05-01.abstract: After two suicide bombers in Israel were found to be British citizens, Britain faced suggestions today that young British Muslims had shifted their focus to terrorism in the Middle East.

2003-05-01.title: Ulster Peace Effort Stumbles Again as Blair Postpones Vote
2003-05-01.abstract: The faltering five-year-old peace effort in Northern Ireland stumbled yet again today when Prime Minister Tony Blair postponed a critical election, blaming the Irish Republican Army for avoiding a "very clear" commitment to end violence.

2003-05-01.title: U.S. Wants to Reopen Talks On Global Anti-Tobacco Pact
2003-05-01.abstract: United States asks officials from 191 countries to reopen negotiations on treaty meant to control sale and use of tobacco; it is scheduled to be adopted month from now at World Health Assembly in Geneva; American negotiators object to treaty's 'no-reservations' clause, which would prevent countries from disregarding any provision they find unacceptable; proposed treaty would place comprehensive ban on advertising and promoting tobacco products, except where prohibition conficts with national constitutions; it would also impose high taxes on tobacco products and expose companies to liability for their products; it would require tobacco companies to divulge all ingredients in their products and print warning labels that cover at least 30 percent of package; it would ban companies from using misleading terms, like ultralight or light; US objection is that certain provisions would override state laws; Democrats in Washington accuse Bush administration of protecting tobacco companies (M)

2003-05-01.title: Of 2,000 Treasures Stolen in Gulf War of 1991, Only 12 Have Been Recovered
2003-05-01.abstract: Experts say more than 2,000 objects were stolen from museums in Iraq in wake of Persian Gulf war in 1991, and not more than half dozen of those pieces have been tracked down; many others are presumed to have been traded away through thriving international market in antiquities; poor record of returning artifacts lost after gulf war suggests daunting obstacles that museum officials and police investigators face as they commit to finding items recently sacked from National Museum of Iraq in Baghdad and other sites; photo; map (M)

2003-05-01.title: A Letter Said to Be From Saddam Hussein Urges Iraqis to Rebel Against the Americans
2003-05-01.abstract: Al Quds al Arabi, London-based Arabic newspaper, prints handwritten letter said to be signed by Saddam Hussein that calls on Iraqis to rise up against American forces, referred to as 'cowardly occupier'; letter was faxed to newspaper's offices and dated Apr 28, Hussein's 66th birthday; newspaper editor Abdel Bari Atwan says letter proves Hussein is still alive and determined to fight back; says he has seen Hussein's signature before and believes letter is 'definitely' his; newspaper describes itself as pan-Arab and independent; it is published in London and New York six times weekly; photo (M)

2003-05-02.title: In Full Flight Regalia, the President Enjoys a 'Top Gun' Moment
2003-05-02.abstract: Pres Bush helps to fly Navy S-3B Viking plane that lands on deck of aircraft carrier Abraham Lincoln; emerges from plane, decked out in flight suit, and is swarmed by cheering members of Lincoln's crew; photo (M)

2003-05-02.title: Kyrenia Journal; Beware These Greeks? No, They're Bearing Cash
2003-05-02.abstract: Greek Cypriots rush to visit Kyrenia after Turkish Cypriots ease travel across buffer zone that had divided island since 1974; many come to celebrate in little port, eating, drinking and shopping; Turks are joining them as though there never was war that tore them apart; photo; map (M)

2003-05-02.title: Monuments Recall Another Empire That Ignored Writing on the Wall
2003-05-02.abstract: Mob violence in Babylon, where Saddam Hussein had built vast palace, is particularly forceful; Babylon once existed as powerful myth, reminder that civilization as we know it was born in region; when Hussein began building there, he reduced its remarkable history to kitsch expression of his ego; photos (M)

2003-05-02.title: Lessons of Anthrax Attacks Help U.S. Respond to SARS
2003-05-02.abstract: United States government is being given high marks for its aggressive handling of SARS, even by critics who had accused senior health officials of bungling response to last major health emergency, anthrax attacks of Oct 2001; Health and Human Services Sec Tommy G Thompson speaks of 'whole new feeling of responsiveness'; most experts say SARS has been less of challenge than anthrax, which came without warning; SARS first emerged in China, giving American health officials time to prepare; unlike Canada, where single SARS patient infected dozens of others, US has apparently had no such 'super spreaders'; Thompson is leaving nothing to chance; gets daily briefings and has met twice with Pres Bush to discuss epidemic; shares concerns of some experts that SARS could wane in warm months, then come roaring back in fall; drawing (M)

2003-05-02.title: World Briefing | Europe: Switzerland: Court Allows Access To Ex-Leader's Files
2003-05-02.abstract: Swiss court denies appeal and makes available former Nigerian dictator Gen Sani Abacha's financial documents; Nigeria is searching for national assets allegedly stolen during Abacha's rule and plans to investigate his Swiss accounts totaling estimated $618 million, which have been frozen since his death; relatives and associates also have interest in ownership of funds and Swiss authorities have demanded assurance from Nigeria that Abacha's widow and sons be treated fairly (S)

2003-05-03.title: U.S. Is Now in Battle for Peace After Winning the War in Iraq
2003-05-03.abstract: American troops, having won war in Iraq, are straining to manage anger, frustration and competing ambitions of nation long suppressed by Saddam Hussein; new politicial parties, Kurds and Shiite religious groups are asserting virtual governmental authority in cities and villages across nation, sometimes under noses of American soldiers; educated Iraqis eager for American-led transformation fear Americans may be losing initiative; anti-American sentiment remains palpable; photos (M

2003-05-03.title: Britain Arrests 6 Linked to Bombing in Israel
2003-05-03.abstract: The six people were arrested in connection with Wednesday's suicide bombing in Tel Aviv that was carried out by a Briton.

2003-05-03.title: An American and 2 Iraqis To Assume Key Oil Posts
2003-05-03.abstract: Former Shell Oil executive Philip J Carroll and two senior Iraqi oil officials are expected to be appointed as top leaders of struggling Iraqi oil sector by American and Iraqi officials; Carroll is expected to be appointed chairman of advisory committee that will have oversight of Iraqi Oil Ministry (M)

2003-05-03.title: SARS Spreads in Taiwan; W.H.O. Plans Trip There
2003-05-03.abstract: SARS appears to be spreading in Taiwan as fast or faster than anywhere except mainland China, prompting World Health Organization to prepare to send team there despite risk of creating diplomatic tensions with Beijing; Taiwan announces that it has 11 more confirmed cases, number that has more than tripled in last 10 days; five people die of SARS in Taiwan, raising its death toll to eight; chart shows outbreak in Taiwan; photo (M)

2003-05-03.title: Iraq's U.S. Overseer Is Praised by Rumsfeld
2003-05-03.abstract: Structure of American administration of postwar Iraq remains unresolved, as Defense Sec Donald Rumsfeld issues strong endorsement of Jay Garner, retired lieutenant general whose job seemed about to be eclipsed by L Paul Bremer (M)

2003-05-04.title: POWELL SAYS SYRIA IS TAKING ACTION ON TERROR GROUPS
2003-05-04.abstract: Sec of State Colin L Powell meets with Pres Bashar al-Assad in Damascus and says Syria has begun closing offices of at least some militant anti-Israeli groups in Damascus as demanded by US; groups are identified as Hamas, Popular Front for Liberation of Palestine-General Command and Palestinian Islamic Jihad; closings appear to be breakthrough in dealing with Syria, which has had an up-and-down relationship with US over years and more recently has been labeled by some American officials as junior member of 'axis of evil' because of its tolerance of groups' presence; Powell also demands that Syria turn over any Iraqi leaders hiding in Syria and ensure that no weapons are flowing from Syria into Iraq now that American occupation is encountering some attacks in parts of country; also expresses concern about Syrian support for Hezbollah, Shiite group that is active in Lebanon and has lobbed missiles into Israel; photo (M)

2003-05-04.title: Bush Says It Will Take Time to Find Iraq's Banned Arms
2003-05-04.abstract: Pres Bush says weapons of mass destruction will be found in Iraq but it will take time because Saddam Hussein spent so many years hiding his stockpile; remarks reflect growing concern in administration that opponents of war will claim that US exaggerated evidence against Iraq in order to justify attack that was intended to depose Hussein; Bush's senior aides have begun backing away from prewar claims that Hussein had arsenal that was loaded and ready to fire, saying that instead he developed 'just in time' production strategy for his weapons, hiding chemical precursors that could be quickly loaded into empty artillery shells or short-range missiles; no evidence has been found that he did so; photo of Bush in Crawford, Tex, with Prime Min John Howard of Australia (M)

2003-05-04.title: Leader of Israel's Labor Party Resigns
2003-05-04.abstract: Amram Mitzna's resignation is a sign of the disarray hobbling Israel's left, but it seems unlikely to affect the new peace process immediately.

2003-05-04.title: A Drifter's Oddysey From the Outback to Guantánamo
2003-05-04.abstract: David Hicks, 27-year-old Australian who converted to Islam, fought with Taliban in Afghanistan and was captured by Northern Alliance forces in Kandahar in December 2001, is now being held at US naval base in Guantanamo, Cuba, and has become thorny issue in US-Australian relations; US, seeking to reduce number of detainees, wants Australia to take custody of Hicks and prosecute him, but Australian authorities are not pressing US for his release and doubt if he can be prosecuted under Australian law; Hicks has only small support group in Australia, but government is criticized in some quarters for abandoning him; his family describes him as rebel searching for a cause; photo (M)

2003-05-04.title: Engine Trouble Suspected in China Submarine Disaster, Newspaper Says
2003-05-04.abstract: The Chinese submarine accident that killed 70 crew members occurred more than two weeks before it was officially revealed, a naval officer has been reported as saying.

2003-05-05.title: U.S. to Free More Afghan War Prisoners From Guantánamo
2003-05-05.abstract: Bush administration officials said today that they would soon release an additional group of prisoners, about a dozen, from the detention center in Guantánamo Bay, Cuba.

2003-05-05.title: Some Fun Returns to Baghdad
2003-05-05.abstract: Iraqis turned their attention to sports and entertainment Sunday, leisure activities that had all but disappeared during the war.

2003-05-05.title: Exile Sees Lessons in Iraqi Grandiosity
2003-05-05.abstract: Kanan Makiya, author and architect who has been among Saddam Hussein's most prominent critics, finally visits infamous monument he wrote about but never saw, called 'crossed swords,' gargantuan archway in Baghada erected as tribute to Hussein and his army in late 1980's; Mikiya says it stands as monument to vulgarization of Iraqi culture under Hussein; photo (M)

2003-05-05.title: Militant Palestinian Groups Dispute Powell's Report of a Crackdown by Damascus
2003-05-05.abstract: Militant Palestinian groups in Damascus challenge American statements that Syria has cracked down on them; Syria's government sidesteps issue, refusing to confirm one of few Syrian concessions that seems to emerge during weekend visit by Secretary of State Colin L Powell (M)

2003-05-05.title: IRAQI WOMEN WARY OF NEW UPHEAVALS
2003-05-05.abstract: Iraqi women view newfound religious freedoms nervously; Iraq does not have history of religious fundamentalism; fear among some women is that conservative Muslim religious leaders will fill power vacuum that has ensued since war ended; Iraq's women enjoyed near parity with men for several decades through 1970's; when Saddam Hussein took power, their rights were curtailed; restrictions he imposed, as well as recent social conservatism, come as blow to older, educated women, who fought against head scarves, arranged marriages and other constraints; their hope is that United States will not allow religious leader to run government; photo (M)

2003-05-06.title: World Briefing | Europe: Serbia: Hague Tribunal Indicts 2
2003-05-06.abstract: Hague war crimes tribunal indicts Jovica Stanisic, Slobodan Milosevic's former secret police chief, and Franko Simatovic, founder of paramilitary group accused of assassinating Serbian Prime Minister Zoran Djindjic (S)

2003-05-06.title: Sales of U.S. Company's Drug Rise as Chinese Try It Against SARS
2003-05-06.abstract: Sales of antiviral drug Zadaxin surge because doctors in China are using it in attempt to contain widening outbreak of SARS; drug is produced by small American biotechnology company SciClone Pharmaceuticals; it is not yet approved for use in United States, Europe or Japan; SciClone president Donald R Sellers says Chinese are probably trying many drugs; Zadaxin is currently being tested by US government scientists, who are screening thousands of compounds in hopes of finding some that may work against SARS (M)

2003-05-06.title: In Victory for Powell, Bush Names Civilian Overseer for Iraq
2003-05-06.abstract: L. Paul Bremer will outrank Jay Garner, the retired Army lieutenant general who has been in charge of postwar administration in Iraq.

2003-05-06.title: World Briefing | Europe: Russia: Monument Honors Sakharov
2003-05-06.abstract: Monument dedicated to Nobel Peace prize winner Andrei D Sakharov is unveiled in St Petersburg; Yelena Bonner, his widow, has opposed tribute stating display is inappropriate given current condition of human rights in Russia; photo (S)

2003-05-06.title: Rebels Execute 10 Hostages In Colombia
2003-05-06.abstract: Colombia's Marxist guerrillas execute provincial governor, former minister of justice and eight others they had been holding hostage in mountains of northern Colombia; victims bodies are found by elite combat troops dropped into heavily forested areas; three soldiers who had also been held captive survive the ordeal; rebels, members of Revolutionary Armed Forces of Colombia (FARC), blame soldiers for touching off violence; rebels continue to hold three American defense contractors as well as dozens of Colombian politicians and soldiers (M)

2003-05-07.title: Study Suggests A Higher Rate Of SARS Death
2003-05-07.abstract: Statistical analysis of 1,425 patients suspected of having SARS who were admitted to Hong Kong hospitals from Feb 20 to Apr 15 suggests death rate from SARS could be up to 55 percent in people 60 and older and 13.2 percent in younger people, for overall rate of 19.9 percent, far higher than health officials had thought; unless numbers fall drastically, SARS would be among infectious diseases with highest death rates; difficulty of calculating mortality rates for newly emerging diseases noted; photo (M)

2003-05-07.title: World Briefing | Europe: Britain: Arrest In American's Killing
2003-05-07.abstract: British authorities arrest 18-year-old man for murder of American artist Margaret Muller; Muller was living in London when she was stabbed to death in busy East London park while on her morning jog; more than 100 witnesses participated in crime reconstruction (S)

2003-05-07.title: U.S. Tests Iraqi Vehicle Suspected as Mobile Weapons Lab
2003-05-07.abstract: The findings were not conclusive as of today, and may not be for some time, but they were nevertheless important, for the White House and the Pentagon.

2003-05-07.title: World Briefing: Europe
2003-05-07.abstract: SPAIN: Work To Resume On Sunken Tanker; BRITAIN: Lawmaker Suspended Over Iraq Remarks; BRITAIN: Arrest In American'S Killing; BRITAIN: Fat Cat;.

2003-05-07.title: Iraqi Documents on Israel Surface on a Cultural Hunt
2003-05-07.abstract: American soldiers from special mobile exploitation team work with members of opposition Iraqi National Congress to search secret police headquarters, Baghdad, for seventh century copy of Jewish Talmud, one of most ancient copies in existence; fail to unearth text, but instead find trove of Iraqi intelligence documents and maps relating to Israel and data on offer by 'holy warrior' to sell uranium and other nuclear materials to Iraq; photo (M)

2003-05-08.title: U.S. Aides Say Iraqi Truck Could Be a Germ-War Lab
2003-05-08.abstract: Senior Bush administration officials say joint British-American team of experts concluded that tractor-trailer truck found in northern Iraq several weeks ago could be mobile biological weapons lab; say it closely fits description given by defector but cannot say whether it has ever produced biological agents for weapons; van was seized Apr 19 at checkpoint controlled by Kurdish allies in northern Iraq; when Secretary of State Colin L Powell addressed United Nations in Feb, he cited defector's report on mobile labs that could develop unconventional weapons and be moved around Iraq to avoid detection and attack; photo (M)

2003-05-08.title: Armitage 'Optimistic' About Renewed India-Pakistan Dialogue
2003-05-08.abstract: Deputy Secretary of State Richard L. Armitage said he was "cautiously optimistic" about the renewal of diplomatic relations between India and Pakistan.

2003-05-08.title: British Rock Star Receives Lesser Punishment in Internet Case
2003-05-08.abstract: Pete Townshend, rock guitarist and co-founder of Who, is given formal police caution and placed on official register of sex offenders for having gained access to pedophile Web site in Jan; court in London clears him of more serious charge of being in possession of indecent pictures downloaded from Internet; police say his name will be removed from register after five years, barring any new incident; he admits using credit card to enter Web site advertising child pornography; claims he was doing research for autobiography that would chronicle his suspected abuse as child; spokesman for group representing child-abuse victims voices outrage at what it considers leniency of Townshend's punishment (M)

2003-05-08.title: Israeli Missile Strike Kills Hamas Militant
2003-05-08.abstract: Israeli attack helicopters fired three missiles that killed a senior Hamas militant driving in Gaza City today, and Israeli troops also shot dead two Palestinians in separate confrontations.

2003-05-08.title: Senate Votes Unanimously to Approve Expansion of NATO
2003-05-08.abstract: President Bush met with the foreign ministers of the seven East European countries and thanked them for their support of American policy on Iraq.

2003-05-09.title: Bush Proposes Free Trade Zone for Middle East
2003-05-09.abstract: President Bush, continuing to wield diplomatic leverage gained from a decisive victory in Iraq, proposed a new free trade zone for the Middle East.

2003-05-09.title: Tourists Seized In the Sahara, But by Whom?
2003-05-09.abstract: Groups of tourists from Europe have been seized in Algerian desert region of Illizi in last few months; their fate, and who took them, are unknown; region is known for drug and gun-smuggling, but it might also provide hideaways for Islamic militant group which has long waged war against Algeria's secular government; in all, 31 tourists are missing: 15 Germans, 10 Austrians, 4 Swiss, a Swede and a Dutchman; no ransom demands have been reported; most of missing people are said to be experienced adventure travelers, equipped with global positioning satellite navigation equipment and satellite telephones; governments involved refuse almost all but most cursory comment; Algeria has reportedly sent 5,000 troops, aided by 300 local guides, to desert to look for missing tourists; Algerian tourism official has been quoted as saying they are alive and that negotiations are taking place to win their release; photo; map (L)

2003-05-09.title: World Briefing | Europe: Britain: 3 Charged In Tel Aviv Bombing
2003-05-09.abstract: Britons Paveen Akthor Sharif, Tahari Shad Tabassum and Zahid Hussain Sharif, detained by British police for possible connection with suicide bombing in Tel Aviv, are charged and will appear in court today; three others being held are released; Tel Aviv bombing was carried out by Asif Hanif, who is from London, and Omar Khan Sharif of Derbyshire; Sharif fled scene when his suicide bomb failed to detonate (S)

2003-05-09.title: U.S. Official Is 'Optimistic' On India-Pakistan Dispute
2003-05-09.abstract: Deputy State Sec Richard L Armitage meets with Pres Pervez Musharraf and other Pakistani officials in Islamabad; then says he is 'cautiously optimistic' about prospect for renewed relations between India and Pakistan; says Musharraf has assured him that infiltration by Islamic militants from Pakistan into part of Kashmir controlled by India has stopped, and that any militant camps found in Pakistan will be closed; India and Pakistan have suprised many with their sudden overtures for peace in last two weeks; Armitage says he hopes to encourage dialogue between two countries over Kashmir (M)

2003-05-09.title: Top Baathist Officials to Be Barred From Government
2003-05-09.abstract: Defense Sec Donald H Rumsfeld says senior Baath Party members will not be allowed to hold positions of authority in postwar Iraq; says Iraqis installed in official jobs may be removed after their initial appointment if subsequent review by American authorities finds official had been senior Baath Party member; Rumsfeld's comments are important because some Iraqis are already complaining that former senior Baath Party officials are being given top jobs in new Iraqi government or in prominent Iraqi institutions; photo (M)

2003-05-10.title: World Briefing: Europe
2003-05-10.abstract: SPAIN: Election Ban On Radical Basque Parties; BRITAIN: Israel Bombing Suspect'S Relatives In Court;.

2003-05-10.title: AFTEREFFECTS: Briefly Noted; U.N. SENDS TEAM TO IRAQ
2003-05-10.abstract: UN sends first team of human rights investigators to Iraq for long-term missionto help distribute aid and handle rights complaints (S)

2003-05-10.title: Colleague Ill, So 30 Toronto Hospital Workers Are in Quarantine
2003-05-10.abstract: Thiry health care workers at Mount Sinai Hospital in Toronto are quarantined after hospital administrator comes down with respiratory symptoms that could be SARS(S)

2003-05-10.title: U.S. Shifts Its Mideast Focus To Press for Easing of Tension
2003-05-10.abstract: US sets aside latest Mideast peace plan for now to press both Israel and Palestinians to take immediate steps to ease tensions; progress doubted as Sec Colin Powell leaves for region (S)

2003-05-10.title: World Briefing | Europe: Britain: Israel Bombing Suspect's Relatives In Court
2003-05-10.abstract: British police disclose identities of three people being held in connection with Tel Aviv (Israel) suicide bombing as sister, brother and wife of Omar Khan Sharif; Sharif is still at large and is wanted by Israeli security forces; bomb he was wearing failed to detonate and he fled scene (S)

2003-05-11.title: Iranian Group Disarms
2003-05-11.abstract: An Iranian opposition group in Iraq agreed to hand over its weapons to the U.S.-led coalition that toppled Saddam Hussein's regime.

2003-05-11.title: Editors' Note
2003-05-11.abstract: Editors' note on New York Times's investigation into reporting of Jayson Blair, who has resigned as reporter after discovery of instances of plagiarism and falsification

2003-05-11.title: American Official Praises India's Move to Defuse Tensions With Pakistan
2003-05-11.abstract: Deputy State Sec Richard L Armitage concludes three-day visit to South Asia, hailing new peace initiative by Indian Prime Min Atal Bihari Vajpayee with neighboring Pakistan as 'far-reaching at of statesmanship' (M)

2003-05-11.title: For Family That Lost 10 to Allied Bomb, Only Memories and Grief Remain
2003-05-11.abstract: Iraqis tell of civilian casualties during weeks of war and allied bombing in Iraq; determining number of civilian casualties is proving difficult; photo (M)

2003-05-11.title: Back in Iraq, A Cleric Urges Islamic Rules
2003-05-11.abstract: Shiite leader Ayatollah Muhammad Bakr al-Hakim returns to Iraq from exile and immediately rejects liberal Western-style democracy as incompatible with country's Islamic culture; says Iraq must base its laws on Islamic strictures; says new Iraqi government must be elected and be representative of all ethnic and religous groups, not only majority Shiites; his emphasis on elected government and national unity reflects marked shift in tone for Shiite leaders after fervent calls for Islamic state in initial euphoria after collapse of Saddam Hussein's regime; Ayatollah Hakim has spent much of his life outside Iraq fighting to overthrow Hussein, who deported and killed thousands of Shiites in relentless campaign to silence dissident religious leaders; he is also one of few senior clerics who openly challenged Hussein and survived assassination, because Iran sheltered and financed him for 23 years; his views are expected to help shape Iraq's political future; photo (M)

2003-05-12.title: Khatami Arrives in Lebanon to Crowds, Chants and Cheers
2003-05-12.abstract: It was the first visit to Beirut by an Iranian president since the 1979 Islamic revolution, and it is being closely watched by the U.S.

2003-05-12.title: Mass Grave Is Unearthed Near Basra
2003-05-12.abstract: Mass grave uncovered near Basra, Iraq, is thought to contain remains of those killed in Shiite Muslim uprisings in 1991 and 1999; family and friends come to site to dig up bones and grieve for dead; photo; map (M)

2003-05-12.title: In Baghdad's Anarchy, the Insane Went Free
2003-05-12.abstract: Iraq's only hospital providing long-term care for chronic schizophrenia and other serious mental disorders was all but destroyed during war, leaving patients to wander off; some got shot and killed, others got raped by looters; of more than 1,400 Iraqis institutionalized at Al Rashad state hospital in Baghdad, 300 remain; Iraqi psychiatric staff accuses marines of standing by as looters carried away everything within hospital; staff says marines then broke door down on maximum security wing and patients fled, untethered from antipsychotic drugs that stabilized many of them; Red Cross has spent $1.5 million over last three years bringing facility almost to Western standards for compassionate care to mentally ill; Dr Olaf Rosset, Norwegian who has overseen project, says he asked US military commanders to send troops to provide security, but they did so only after hospital was all but destroyed; psychiatrists at hospital meet with desperate Iraqis who plead for help in handling disturbed family members who returned home after escaping from hospital; photo (L)

2003-05-12.title: Lessons From Iraq Include How to Scare Korean Leader
2003-05-12.abstract: American intelligence officials conclude that North Korean leader Kim Jong Il went into seclusion during final buildup to war in Iraq because he feared he too might be target of attack; Pentagon is considering new ways to hold him and his inner circle at risk as way of bolstering deterrence on peninsula; military's ability to mount precision attacks on leadership targets in Iraq is being examined to see how it might apply in tense standoff with North Korea, perhaps influencing North Korea's behavior without ever firing shot; photo (M)

2003-05-12.title: Iraqis More Bemused Than Enthused by Cleric
2003-05-12.abstract: Ayatollah Muhammad Bakr al-Hakim, longtime opponent of Saddam Hussein who spent last 32 years in exile in Iran, is expected to enter Najaf and assert his claim to speak for Iraq's long-suppressed Shiites in negotiations for postwar government; his return to Iraq has not generated much excitement so far; many Iraqis are especially suspicious of former exiles who spent years outside country and have now returned to help organize transitional goverment; photo (M)

2003-05-13.title: 41 Die in Suicide Bombing in Chechnya
2003-05-13.abstract: Truck loaded with explosives blows up near government complex in northern Chechnya, killing at least 41 people and wounding scores more; officials call it suicidal terrorist attack; region has until now escaped worst of Russia's war against Chechen separatists; none of Chechnya's known separatist groups claims responsibilty for attack; it is worst violence there since Russia held constitutional referendum in March as part of Kremlins's efforts to hasten peaceful end to war after 44 months of violence; Pres Vladimir V Putin says attack is intended to disrupt efforts to foster stability in Chechnya under loose political federation with Russia; photo; map (M)

2003-05-13.title: EXPLOSIONS ROCK WESTERN ENCLAVES IN SAUDI CAPITAL
2003-05-13.abstract: Four separate overnight bombing attacks strike Western targets in Riyadh, Saudi Arabia, on eve of Secretary of State Colin L Powell's visit there, causing undetermined number of deaths and dozens of injuries; United States Embassy in Riyadh says 44 Americans are injured, some seriously; local press reports deaths of one Saudi, one Lebanese and one Westerner; casualty figures are expected to rise; Powell says violence is likely work of Al Qaeda, saying it bears its hallmarks; says Al Qaeda has been weakened but not destroyed; live pictures from scene attest to power of blasts at apartment compounds that are home to American, British, Italian and other Westerners, as well as to Saudis and citizens of other Middle Eastern countries; Saudi Interior Min Prince Nayef says first three blasts were suicide bombings set off by cars stuffed with explosives driven into targeted compounds; attackers strike after State Dept's extraordinarily specific warning on May 1 that terrorists would soon attack American targets in Saudi Arabia; there was botched attempt by Saudi security services to seize cell that Interior Ministry accused of being linked to Al Qaeda terrorist network; 19 suspected militants escaped, but police seized huge arms cache; photos; map (L)

2003-05-13.title: Sharon Sets Hard Line on Settlements Policy
2003-05-13.abstract: The Israeli prime minister dismissed as not "on the horizon" any talk of changing settlement policy.

2003-05-13.title: U.S. Official Treads Carefully In Overseeing Iraq Oil Industry
2003-05-13.abstract: Philip J Carroll, American official charged by Bush administration with broad oversight of Iraqi oil industry, says he understands that his appointment is viewed by many Iraqis and others in Arab world as American grab for country's vast oil wealth; says he will take initial approach of learning from Iraqis already working there and of keeping low profile; says he will try to calm concerns among members of Orgn of Petroleum Exporting Countries that Iraq, under American pressure, would abandon cartel; says Iraq's membership is not for US government to decide; photo (M)

2003-05-13.title: U.N. Council May Request Foreign Force For Congo
2003-05-13.abstract: Security Council ambassadors discuss possibility of inviting foreign military force to help avert any increase in violence in Ituri province of Democratic Republic of Congo, where power vacuum has emerged since troops from neighboring Uganda withdrew (M)

2003-05-14.title: Former Argentine Leader Still in Presidential Race
2003-05-14.abstract: Former Pres Carlos Menem reportedly withdraws from Argentine runoff election and then retracts resignation after aides convince him not to give up uphill battle for third term; withdrawal would give Nestor Kirchner victory by default (S)

2003-05-14.title: Sharon Rules Out Dismantling Settlements
2003-05-14.abstract: Prime Min Ariel Sharon dismisses idea of restraining Israeli settlements and rejects suggestions that US is pressuring him to do so to promote new peace plan (M)

2003-05-14.title: Experimental Drug May Fight SARS, Researchers Say
2003-05-14.abstract: German researchers say that experimental Pfizer drug AG-7088, which was tested and shelved as common cold remedy, may, with some chemical tinkering, be used to fight SARS virus; Pfizer did not apply for FDA approval and modification would take several years; researcher Dr David Ho says in Hong Kong that he and colleages at Aaron Diamond AIDS Research Center in New York have synthesized several peptides that could prevent SARS from entering human cells, but that will also take years of testing (M)

2003-05-14.title: Powell Gets Warm Words in Moscow but No Deal on Iraq
2003-05-14.abstract: President Vladimir V. Putin and Secretary of State Colin L. Powell met in the Kremlin and failed to resolve most of their differences involving the future of Iraq.

2003-05-14.title: World Briefing: Europe
2003-05-14.abstract: GERMANY: Neo-Nazi Crime Up; GERMANY: Bird Flu Confirmed; ITALY: Berlusconi Says He Will Sue Over Insults; IRELAND: Landmark Saved;.

2003-05-15.title: Kurdish Group Takes Autonomous Role in Iraq Oil Projects
2003-05-15.abstract: Patriotic Union of Kurdistan, Kurdish political party working with United States to shape interim government in Iraq, pushes ahead quietly on three oil development projects, acting autonomously as local government; party has signed production-sharing contracts with two Turkish companies to develop and survey oilfields in northeast Iraq, and has agreed to allow Australian company to do surveying work in eastern Iraq; photo (M)

2003-05-15.title: U.N. Vote on Iraq Authority Is Due Next Week, U.S. Says
2003-05-15.abstract: United States Amb John D Negroponte says he expects Security Council to vote next week on new draft resolution lifting sanctions and recognizing forces that defeated and occupied Iraq as legal authority in country; photo (M)

2003-05-15.title: Bremer Says U.S. Is Tackling Security Problem in Baghdad
2003-05-15.abstract: About 300 violent criminals have been arrested in the last 48 hours as part of a security clampdown, the new American administrator for Iraq said today.

2003-05-15.title: Panel Rejects Nuclear Arms Of Small Yield
2003-05-15.abstract: Proposal by Bush administration to allow development of new kinds of small nuclear weapons is rejected by Republican-controlled House Armed Services Committee (S)

2003-05-15.title: 17 Tourists Abducted in Sahara Are Freed in Army Raid
2003-05-15.abstract: Seventeeen European tourists held captive in Sahara in Algeria are released unharmed after Algerian armed forces raid camp said to be controlled by militant Islamic group; 15 other travelers missing since Mar are still unaccounted for; senior German official terms their situation 'precarious'; freed tourists--10 Austrians, 6 Germans and Swede--were held by members of Salafist Group for Preaching and Combat; group has been fighting guerrilla war against secular Algerian government and is beleived to have ties to Al Qaeda; freed captives appear healthy as they arrive home; it is unclear whether there is any connection between their release and recent visit to Algiers of German Foreign Min Joschka Fischer; photos; map (M)

2003-05-16.title: Germans Edge Toward U.S. Stance on U.N. Iraq Resolution
2003-05-16.abstract: Germany's top officials said today that they wanted to reach an agreement on the U.N. resolution on Iraq that is being pressed by Britain and the United States.

2003-05-16.title: Number of Cases in Taiwan May Be More Than Reported
2003-05-16.abstract: World Health Orgn officials say outbreak of SARS in Taiwan is being inaccurately counted and is probably much bigger than reported; official Taiwan tally is 264 cases with 34 deaths; Dr Lee Ming-liang, former health minister leading fight against epidemic, says Taiwan would conform to WHO standards in reporting outbreak, and that when it does, there may be many more probable cases; disease has spread to another major hospital in Taipei, infecting 7 doctors and nurses said to be wearing full infection gear, and ambulance drivers, one of whom dies (M)

2003-05-16.title: Putin Offers an Amnesty Plan Covering Most Chechen Rebels
2003-05-16.abstract: Pres Vladimir V Putin of Russia offers to grant amnesty to Chechen separatists who agree to lay down their arms by Aug 1; offer excludes any accused of murder, rape, kidnapping or other crimes; proposal comes after two suicide attacks in Chechnya this week killed at least 77 people and shattered Kremlin's latest assertions that peace and stability in republic are at hand after 44 months of war; proposal, submitted to lower house of Parliament for vote, appears to be effort to regain political initiative that Putin administration hoped to sustain after voters approved new constitution for Chechnya in Mar; Putin calls amnesty proposal 'an act of humanism'; it remains to be seen whether offer will entice significant numbers of Chechen fighters to accept return to civilian life under Russian rule (M)

2003-05-16.title: Five Requests to Saudis Went Unheeded, U.S. Says
2003-05-16.abstract: Bush administration officials say that in month before bombings in Riyadh, United States asked Saudi Arabia on at least five separate occasions to deploy armed uniformed government guards around all Western targets of possible terrorist attack; say one request was made by Stephen J Hadley, deputy national security adviser, in May 5 meeting with Crown Prince Abdullah, kingdom's day-to-day leader; say Saudi authorities did nothing to augment security at any of sites; Saudi ambassador to US Prince Bandar bin Sultan says Saudi security agencies concluded that they had adequate measures in place; notes near impossibility of deterring 'determined, crazy, evil person'; other Saudi officials point out that there are hundreds of potential Western targets in kingdom; American officials acknowledge that at no time did US specify which sites it wanted guarded; say precise nature of attacks in Riyadh raises questions about their assumption that they had successfully reined in Al Qaeda (M)

2003-05-16.title: Lebanon Says Arrests Foiled Attack on the U.S. Embassy
2003-05-16.abstract: Lebanese government officials say Lebanese Army, helped by Syrian intelligence, arrested nine Islamic militants in northern Lebanon who were plotting attack on United States Embassy in Beirut; say men were also planning to take one or more government officials hostage as bargaining chip in efforts to free 30 militants of Sunni Muslim extremist movement arrested in government crackdown two years ago; Army spokesman says movement, Redemption and Dawn of Islam, adheres to strict Wahhabi branch dominant in Saudi Arabia; says 7 of men are Lebanese and two Palestinians, and that explosives and detonators were also seized during arrests (M)

2003-05-17.title: Suicide Bombs Kill at Least 14 In Casablanca
2003-05-17.abstract: At least 14 people are killed in several explosions in Casablanca, Morocco, as attackers use car bombs and detonate another device to strike Jewish, Spanish and Belgian targets; Interior Min Mostafa Sahel tells reporters that attacks 'bear the hallmark of international terrorism'; says that 10 suicide bombers were also killed anddddthat 60 other people were injured (M)

2003-05-17.title: Bicyclists near end of Cairo-to-Cape Town race : 4 long months on 2 wheels
2003-05-17.abstract: It is called the Tour d'Afrique and, like the Tour de France, it is a grueling, multiple-stage bicycle race.

2003-05-17.title: Saudis Triple Bomb Inquiry; Vow Joint Antiterror Effort
2003-05-17.abstract: Some 70 more American law enforcement and intelligence officials leave for Saudi Arabia after Saudi government approves tripling size of team probing terrorist bombing attacks on three Western residential complexes in Riyadh; Saudi official Adel al-Jubeir calls bombings 'massive jolt,' news conference, Washington; says government will work with United States and do whatever is needed to halt internal terrorism; admits government had not responded in days before attacks to urgent American requests for tightening of security around Western compounds; details aggressive and determined Saudi effort against terrorism; holds nation is being unfairly tarred by critics, including anonymous Bush administration officials; Federal Bureau of Investigation Dir Robert Mueller and Atty Gen John Ashcroft laud initial Saudi probe; photo (M)

2003-05-17.title: Dowry Too High. Lose Bride and Go to Jail.
2003-05-17.abstract: Nisha Sharma becomes overnight sensation in India by calling police to her wedding and having groom arrested after free-for-all between their families over his family's last-minute demand for larger dowry; dowries, illegal for many decades in India, are now often disguised by families as gifts to give newlyweds a start in life; more than a media creation, Sharma and her dowry defiance strike chord in nation, whose expanding middle class is rebelling against dowry tradition that is being overfed by new commercialism; photo (M)

2003-05-17.title: World Briefing | Europe: Spain: Anger At Broadcast
2003-05-17.abstract: Spanish Deputy Prime Minister Mariano Rajoy expresses government's anger over video of ETA, Basque separatist group, in which three hooded members urge viewers to vote for outlawed candidates; video was aired on Basque public television station Euskal Irrati Telebista and is under investigation by state prosecutor (S)

2003-05-18.title: Euro Beginning to Flex Its Economic Muscles
2003-05-18.abstract: Euro is growing into rival to dollar, making up all ground it lost after its introduction in 1999; more important, it has gained stature as safe haven for investors and governments; with US running up vast deficits, economists say euro has chance to consolidate gains; photo; charts show growth of euro against dollar and as percentage of world's foreign exchange reserves (M)

2003-05-18.title: His Face Still Gives Fits As Saddam Dinar Soars
2003-05-18.abstract: Iraq's old currency, so-called Saddam dinar, skyrockets in value, soaring by about one-third against dollar, and is now at highest value since 1996; bill bears portrait of former dictator Saddam Hussein; dinar's surge along with dollar's plunge have wiped out purchasing power of $20 emergency payments being handed out to workers, who have been grumbling that it was poor substitute for pay, which they have not received since March; photo (M)

2003-05-18.title: In Bus Attack, a Jerusalem Suicide Bomber Kills 6 and Wounds More Than 20
2003-05-18.abstract: Suicide bomber attacks bus in Jerusalem, killing at least six people and wounding more than 20; attack comes hours after Israeli Prime Min Ariel Sharon and Palestinian Prime Min Mahmoud Abbas hold talks; Sharon cancels trip to US, during which he was scheduled to see Pres Bush; other violence, which is thought to be in response to peace efforts, described; second blast occurs on outskirts of city, but no once except bomber is killed; police say bus bomber was disguised as observant Jew; no claim is made for bombing; photo (M)

2003-05-18.title: Iraqi Shiite Group Criticizes U.S. Civilian Administrator
2003-05-18.abstract: The group said the administrator, L. Paul Bremer III, has failed to support the rapid creation of an Iraqi-led interim government.

2003-05-18.title: As AIDS Ravages Caribbean, Governments Confront Denial
2003-05-18.abstract: AIDS is imperiling many Caribbean countries as it spreads into population; infection rate there is highest outside Africa; health experts and political leaders warn of potential for devastation in region of small, image-conscious countries that depend on limited pool of labor and resources, as well as tourism; 40,000 adults and children in Caribbean are believed to have died of disease in 2001 alone; it is leading cause of death among young men; epidemic's full extent is obscured by fear, denial, limited treatment and lack of public health resources; estimated 2.4 percent of Caribbean's adult population is thought to be infected with HIV; migration between islands--and United States--is common and helps spread disease; medications remain unavailable in many places, and on many islands hospitals and doctors lack will or resources to treat HIV-positive patients, bouncing them from clinic to clinic; photos; map; chart (L)

2003-05-19.title: In Casablanca Neighborhood, Ruptured Calm
2003-05-19.abstract: Speculation grows that suicide bombing attacks in Casablanca at five separate sites that killed 41 people, including 13 bombers, might have blended order from abroad with homegrown organization; Moroccan authorities are calling attacks highly coordinated terrorist operation linked to sophisticated international terrorist network; but fact that suicide bombers' likely target, sprawling Jewish cemetery, escaped harm, and that three young Muslim men are among victims, lends amateur quality to events; King Mohammed VI arrives in Casablanca to show his direct involvement in aftermath of attacks; stops at several bombed sites, including hotel, Jewish cultural center and private Spanish club; says he has asked Interior Ministry and security services to investigate 'in full transparency' and to keep public informed of developments; photos (M)

2003-05-19.title: Israelis Say 2 Bombers Appeared to Be a Team
2003-05-19.abstract: Israeli officials and Palestinians say two Palestinian suicide bombers who killed themselves and nine other people in explosions 12 hours apart over weekend in Hebron and Jerusalem appear to have been working in concert; note bombers lived block apart in Hebron, belonged to militant Hamas movement and carried out their attacks disguised as religiously observant Jews; in Hebron, single mourning tent is set up for two bombers, and coffee and candy is handed out to friends and relatives who gather; Israeli troops surround street and order people to leave; Palestinian information min Nabil Amr condemns bombings (M)

2003-05-19.title: Thousands of Iraqis Rally to Protest U.S. Moves in Baghdad
2003-05-19.abstract: The rally combined a religious event with a political protest against the American military presence and Washington's plans for a future Iraqi government.

2003-05-19.title: Massaciuccoli Journal; The Killer From Louisiana, on the Loose in Italy
2003-05-19.abstract: Louisiana crawfish, brought to Lake Massaciuccoli in Tuscany more than decade ago as culinary experiment, has multiplied greatly, wreaking havoc on native crawfish and throwing life in lake out of whack; biologists have deployed thousands of pike into lake to combat invader crawfish; photos; map (M)

2003-05-19.title: Cuban Dissident Says Castro Uses Invasion Fear as Ploy
2003-05-19.abstract: Cuba's leading dissident today faulted the Communist government on the island for manipulating fears of an American invasion to justify its harsh line on opposition.

2003-05-20.title: Bush Insists Mideast Peace Plan Will Move Forward, Despite Bombings
2003-05-20.abstract: Pres Bush defends administration's peace plan for Middle East as aides scramble to revive Israeli-Palestinian discussions in face of five bomb attacks on Israelis in three days; says road to peace will be bumpy; despite his hopeful words, some administration officials concede that little can be done to sustain delicate momentum of peace effort if suicide bombings of last few days continue; say their first priority is to keep pressing Palestinian Prime Minister Mahmoud Abbas to take action against Hamas, Aksa Martyrs Brigades and other groups that have taken responsibility for bombing attacks against Israel; Prime Min Ariel Sharon of Israel is coming under pressure from conservatives in his cabinet to act against Yasir Arafat; State Dept says it is still American policy to discourage Israel from expelling Arafat from West Bank; photos (M)

2003-05-20.title: 3 ISRAELIS KILLED AND 50 WOUNDED IN BLAST AT MALL
2003-05-20.abstract: Fifth Palestinian suicide bomber to strike against Israelis in less than 48 hours detonates shrapnel-packed bomb outside mall in northern town of Afula, killing security guard and two bystanders, wounding more than 50 others and delivering new blow to peace effort that is already reeling; blast so disfigures dead that hours later authorities are still trying to identify bomber among remains; police say they believe bomber was woman; latest attack is part of what Israeli police call 'wave of terror' that has greeted most intensive international effort to date to end 31-month conflict; Pres Bush says new international place plan 'still stands,' but Israeli officials say violence has strengthened their resolve to substantially change plan, which lays out reciprocal concessions to achieve peace and Palestinian state in three years; senior Israeli security official says new attacks are aimed both at Israel and at Mahmoud Abbas, first Palestinian prime minister, who has promised to disarm militant groups; photos (L)

2003-05-20.title: No Cases of SARS Have Been Transmitted on Airlines Since March, W.H.O. Reports
2003-05-20.abstract: World Health Orgn reports no cases of SARS have been transmitted on airline flights since late Mar, after it recommended that countries and airlines screen passengers and crew members departing from affected areas; says most recent affected flight was on Mar 23, from Bangkok to Beijing, four days before it made its recommendations on air travel; Taiwan, which is battling epidemic of SARS, is unsuccessful in its effort to use disease as reason to gain observer status in WHO; photo (M)

2003-05-20.title: Final Arguments Start in Trial Of 4 Arabs in Terrorism Case
2003-05-20.abstract: Closing arguments begin in Detroit in trial of four Arabs arrested after Sept 11, 2001 attacks; prosecutors say four plotted terrorism; defense tries to cast doubt on government's case and its crucial witness Youssef Hmimssa, onetime associate of defendants; they are charged with document fraud, conspiracy and material support of terrorism; they are Farouk ali-Haimoud of Algeria and Abdel-Ilah Elmardoudi, Ahmed Hannan and Karim Koubriti of Morocco (M)

2003-05-20.title: Names of the Dead
2003-05-20.abstract: Dept of Defense releases latest list of confirmed deaths of Americans in Iraq (S)

2003-05-21.title: U.S. Forces in Kabul Shoot Afghan Soldiers by Mistake
2003-05-21.abstract: American marines guarding the U.S. Embassy in Kabul shot and killed at least three Afghan soldiers from the Intelligence Service today and wounded two more.

2003-05-21.title: U.S. Suggests a Qaeda Cell in Iran Directed Saudi Bombings
2003-05-21.abstract: US intercepts communications strongly suggesting that small cell of Al Qaeda leaders in Iran directed recent terrorist attacks in Saudi Arabia, and US lodges strong protest with Iran; US is said to have hard intelligence that at least dozen Qaeda figures are in Iran, including Saif al-Adel and Osama bin Laden's son, Saad bin Laden; Sec Donald Rumsfeld comments; photos (M)

2003-05-21.title: U.S. Analysts Link Iraq Labs To Germ Arms
2003-05-21.abstract: US intelligence agencies conclude that two mysterious trailers found in Iraq were mobile units to produce germs for weapons, but have not found biological agents or evidence of such use; trailers were thoroughly decontaminated with unidentified caustic agent but experts can come up with no other plausible explanation; US reportedly will invite international experts to examine mobile units; photos; diagram; Iraqi scientists claim 2,000-liter vessel in each unit was used to mix chemicals to produce hydrogen gas, but evidence suggests vessels, made in 2002 and 2003, are fermenters used to multiply seed germs into deadly swarms (M)

2003-05-21.title: U.N. Begins Effort in Iraq To Mend Health Services
2003-05-21.abstract: World Health Organization begins program to restore basic health services in Iraq, at estimated cost of up to $180 million; Dr David Nabarro explans plans (S)

2003-05-21.title: World Briefing: Americas
2003-05-21.abstract: ARGENTINA: DEPARTING LEADER PARDONS PLOTTERS. VENEZUELA: PAGEANT REPRIEVE.

2003-05-22.title: Tales of Iraqis' Silent Suffering Now Find Voices
2003-05-22.abstract: Cemetery in Najaf, Iraq, expanded to eight times its previous size in one decade to accommodate casualties of war and repression during Saddam Hussein's rule; people of Najaf are only now able to recount events of those years to outsiders, about bloodshed they witnessed and bullet-torn bodies they helped bury in unmarked graves; one keeper of graveyard describes 'days of chaos' in 1991, when Shiite Muslims launched rebellion against Hussein and Iraqi soldiers pursued them into cemetery, taking 15 days to find them and shoot them to death; photos; map (M)

2003-05-22.title: U.S. Moving to Clamp Down on Crime in Baghdad
2003-05-22.abstract: The military is deploying one of its most highly regarded armored divisions in Baghdad in an effort to bring greater order.

2003-05-22.title: Marines at U.S. Embassy Kill 3 Afghan Soldiers in Incident Called Error
2003-05-22.abstract: American marines guarding United States Embassy in Kabul shoot and kill at least three Afghan soldiers from Intelligence Service and wound two others, in what American officials call regrettable incident; Afghan soldiers and officers say more men were wounded as they rushed to help first colleagues who were hit; say their battalion commander was seriously wounded; there are no American casualties; shootings occur after American marines in Kabul are placed on high alert amid warnings of imminent terrorist attacks around world; photo (M)

2003-05-22.title: Tolosa Journal; Politics in a Perilous Place: The Basque Country
2003-05-22.abstract: Voters in Basque town of Tolosa prepare to vote for candidates of outlawed Batasuna party, which has been linked by Spanish government and United States to violent separatist group ETA; hope is that ballots, although void, will be counted unofficially and reveal strength of popular support for Batasuna; photos; map (M)

2003-05-22.title: World Briefing: Europe
2003-05-22.abstract: BRITAIN: CHILD KILLER GRANTED LIFELONG ANONYMITY. IRELAND: PARTY DEFENDS ITSELF.

2003-05-23.title: Algerians Begin Burying Dead From Quake; Death Toll Rises
2003-05-23.abstract: More than 1,600 were killed in the earthquake that struck Algeria, "and the victim figures are expected to rise," Prime Minister Ahmed Ouyahia said at a news conference today.

2003-05-23.title: Alejandro DeTomaso, 74, Maker of Sports Cars, Dies
2003-05-23.abstract: Alejandro DeTomaso, whose company DeTomaso Automobili produced sports car that combined Italian flair with American horsepower, dies at age 74; photos (M)

2003-05-23.title: U.S. Seeking Guantánamo Defense Staff
2003-05-23.abstract: Pentagon is having trouble recruiting civilian lawyers to represent terrorist suspects in its military tribunals at United States naval base in Guantanamo Bay, Cuba; Pres Bush has not singled out any prisoner from Afghanistan for tribunal yet, but Pentagon has been preparing for one; suspects will be provided military defense lawyers but can also ask for civilian lawyers; Col Will A Gunn, named as acting chief defense counsel, says he has had some inquiries from military lawyers but none from civilian lawyers (M)

2003-05-23.title: British Give Port Control To the Iraqis
2003-05-23.abstract: British military hands over major Iraqi port of Umm Qasr to local administrators in symbolic transfer of power intended to show that Iraqis are beginning to take control of major elements of their country; British military prepares to scale back its personnel at port, which is chief gateway for aid agencies bringing supplies into country; new Iraqi Port Authority for Umm Qasr will remain under oversight of American and British administrators now running country (M)

2003-05-23.title: Some Chinese Say Government's Response to Epidemic Has Been Too Heavy-Handed
2003-05-23.abstract: China's Communist Party seeks to claim credit for defusing SARS crisis caused partly by official denial and deceit; some scholars and media commentators, disagree, saying SARS did more to expose weaknesses of China's political culture than its strengths; frank debate about government's handling of SARS comes as anxiety about disease has begun to ease (M)

2003-05-24.title: World Briefing: Asia
2003-05-24.abstract: CHINA: Missile Penalties Criticized; PAKISTAN: Billboards Torn Down; INDIA: Heat Wave Kills Scores;.

2003-05-24.title: Lifting of Iraq Sanctions Ends 13 Years of Isolation, but Immediate Relief Isn't Expected
2003-05-24.abstract: End to United Nations trade sanctions will officially end 13 years of international isolation for Iraq; experts say move will bring little immediate relief to Iraq's battered economy; say biggest restraint on trade and investment has been fear about current lawlessness and instability, not sanctions (M)

2003-05-24.title: Kabul Announces Push to Gain Revenue and Combat Corruption
2003-05-24.abstract: Afghani Min Ashraf Ghani sets aggressive new drive to overhaul customs and finance departments countrywide and curb corruption in bid to draw in revenue for central treasury; outlines plan after 12 most powerful regional leaders agree to hand over their customs revenue and income from state enterprises in their areas; says he will visit Herat, where Gen Ismail Khan draws in estimated $400 million a year in customs; plan also puts spotlight on lucrative enterprises controlled by Gen Abdul Rashid Dostum in north (M)

2003-05-24.title: Iraq's Liquor Trade Becomes A Casualty of Postwar Chaos
2003-05-24.abstract: Attacks by Islamic activists are wiping out liquor stores in Iraq, particularly in Basra and other southern cities where Shiite Muslims are in majority; clerics and Islamic political parties blame zealots beyond their control (M)

2003-05-24.title: World Briefing | Europe: The Netherlands: Smuggling Trial Begins
2003-05-24.abstract: Seven men and one woman go on trial in Netherlands for alleged involvement in Chinese smuggling operation; are believed to have been responsible for deaths of 58 Chinese immigrants found in truck in Dover (England) three years ago (S)

2003-05-25.title: Tales of Black Ninjas Are Spreading Fear in Rural Thailand
2003-05-25.abstract: Rumors of evildoers dressed in black are latest wave of panic sweeping through largely Muslim provinces of southern Thailand, where central government in overwhelmingly Buddhist nation has failed to reduce widespread poverty and disaffection; mob of rural villagers attack and kill two plainclothes policemen after rumor spreads that they were black ninjas (M)

2003-05-25.title: Argentina Looks to a New Leader
2003-05-25.abstract: Nestor Kirchner, who takes office as president of Argentina on May 25, faces economic and political crisis that has consumed five presidents in 18 months; demands for new direction are more insistent than ever; economy has begun to bounce back after hitting bottom, leaving a fifth of work force unemployed and thousands of businesses bankrupt; photos (M)

2003-05-25.title: Raw Iraqi Art Breaks Free of Chokehold
2003-05-25.abstract: Art exhibit is held in damaged government building in Basra, Iraq, and artists show their first works created free from controls of Saddam Hussein; photo (M)

2003-05-25.title: U.S. Detains 5 Suspected Baath Loyalists at Kirkuk Elections
2003-05-25.abstract: Leading figures in Kirkuk, Iraq, gather to elect City Council; American authorities detain five delegates who were to take part in voting after receiving intelligence reports that men had been high-ranking members of Saddam Hussein's Baath Party; American authorities have begun organizing elections in northern Iraq--first in Mosul and now Kirkuk; Americans say elections, which are not popular ballots but are carried out by assembly of local leaders choosing interim administrations, are first step toward Iraqis governing themselves (M)

2003-05-25.title: Mobster's Release Brings Criticism of Italian Policy on Informers
2003-05-25.abstract: Early release from prison of mobster convicted of murder touches off furious debate in Italy about policies that reward even worst killers if they provide information that helps put other members of Mafia behind bars; Enzo Brusca, whose prison sentence was commuted to house arrest, is one of more than 1,100 criminal informers, called pentiti, who are collaborating with Italian law enforcement officials, and they have become favorite weapons Italian police use against criminal organizations (M)

2003-05-26.title: Five Are Arrested in Reported Plan to Set Off Bomb in Kandahar
2003-05-26.abstract: Afghan authorities arrest five men who allegedly planned to bomb busy area in Kandahar; Mullah Janan, Taliban member with close ties to Al Qaeda, is among those arrested (M)

2003-05-26.title: Turkish Cypriot Publisher Goes to Press, and Court, Almost Daily
No Mad Cow Disease in Herd That Had Infected Animal
2003-05-26.abstract: Nearly 200 cows belonging to the same Alberta herd as the cow infected with mad cow disease were found to be free of the disease, according to test results made public over the weekend.

2003-05-26.title: Aznar's Party Keeps Hold on Madrid in Vote
2003-05-26.abstract: Spanish Prime Min Jose Maria Aznar's governing party loses some ground in municipal and regional elections; manages to hold power in Madrid and many other cities; opposition Socialists win largest number of votes nationwide for first time in decade, taking control of Madrid's regional government; Aznar's wife, Ana Botella, is elected to Madrid town council (M)

2003-05-26.title: U.S. Demands That Iran Turn Over Qaeda Agents And Join Saudi Inquiry
2003-05-26.abstract: Bush administration officials say US is pressing Iran to cooperate with investigation into recent bombings in Riyadh, Saudi Arabia, and to hand over operatives of Al Qaeda believed to have been working in Iranian territory; administration has held series of secret meetings with Iranian officials, who continue to deny any involvement with Al Qaeda; UN Amb Javad Zarif says Tehran is cooperating in attempts to control Al Qaeda and has arrested members of network but would not respond to 'language of pressure'; dispute in intelligence community about Iran-Al Qaeda connection discussed; some in administration believe terrorists could be in Iran but not necessarily with sanction of government; there is also concern that Iran is supporting Shiite groups in Iraq seeking to destabilize American occupation; possible acceleration by Iran of its nuclear weapons program discussed; photo (M)

2003-05-27.title: Gunmen Kill 2 U.S. Soldiers in a Firefight in Central Iraq
2003-05-27.abstract: Unidentified gunmen fired machine guns and rocket-propelled grenades today at a column of military vehicles, killing two American soldiers.

2003-05-27.title: Congo War Toll Soars as U.N. Pleads for Aid
2003-05-27.abstract: Latest massacre in Congo reveals, with graphic and embarrassing detail, impotence of international reaction to horrors that have befallen that country; death toll in town of Bunia stands at 350 as militias belonging to rival Hema and Lendu tribes battle for control; machete's ghastly wreckage is evident on men, women and children inside what passes for hospital; there is no telling when next round of carnage will unfold, or whether United Nations Security Council will send troops to bring order; Secretary Gen Kofi Annan has appealed to member countries to send soldiers for multinational force; peacekeepers sent to Bunia were overrun and two of them were also massacred; American officials say only long-lasting solution for peace in Congo is to persuade outsiders, namely Uganda and Rwanda, to stop arming proxy fighters and to support peace deal recently negotiated between warring factions; photos; map (M)

2003-05-27.title: Tests in China Suggest Some With SARS Don't Become Ill
2003-05-27.abstract: World Health Orgn reports that blood tests of people who work with exotic animals in markets in southern China show that significant proportion apparently have been infected with SARS virus, suggesting that some people may become infected without becoming ill; findings are from two separate studies of workers in markets in Guangdong Province; they strengthen SARS link between animals and humans (M)

2003-05-27.title: A Return to North Carolina, Marred by Loss of 2 Sailors
2003-05-27.abstract: Amphibious assault ship Nassau sails into North Carolina waters after nine months at sea and begins to disembark 1,100 marines returning to Camp Lejeune, part of first contingent home from war in Iraq; arrival is delayed by searches for two missing sailors; one fell into ocean chasing football on deck and second did not show up for roll call (M)

2003-05-27.title: World Briefing | Europe: Italy: Berlusconi Party Lagging In Rome Race
2003-05-27.abstract: Some analysts contend local Italian elections reflect mixed feelings about Prime Minister Silvio Berlusconi's government as early results predict his party losing Province of Rome to center-left opposition; Berlusconi has been embroiled in corruption trial (S)

2003-05-28.title: Saudis Arrest at Least 8 Men Believed Tied to Bombings
2003-05-28.abstract: The Saudi police made the arrests, which included the accused mastermind, in a series of raids in and around Medina.

2003-05-28.title: SARS Forces Beijing to Fight An Old but Unsanitary Habit
2003-05-28.abstract: China's battle against SARS virus takes on old unsanitary habit of spitting in street, with authorities and volunteers handing out little plastic spit bags in parks and malls; photos; Communist Party Central Committee gives imprimatur to campaign, issuing Directive on Launching Activities to Transform Vile Habits (M)

2003-05-28.title: Canada Introduces Measure Adjusting Penalties for Marijuana
2003-05-28.abstract: Canadian government introduces legislation to decriminalize possession of small amounts of marijuana but set stricter penalties for trafficking in drug (S)

2003-05-28.title: Rumsfeld Echoes Notion That Iraq Destroyed Arms
2003-05-28.abstract: Sec Donald H Rumsfeld, responding to questions after speech to Council on Foreign Relations, New York City, suggests publicly for first time that Iraq might have destroyed chemical and biological weapons before war; again holds it is just matter of time before military teams uncover illicit arms Pres Bush cited as major reason for war; seeks to counter critics who have accused administration of bungling post-war campaign in Iraq; appeals for patience for reconstruction effort that will be difficult and bumpy at times; photo (M)

2003-05-28.title: A Saudi Editor Who Offended Clerics Is Ousted From His Post
2003-05-28.abstract: Saudi Arabia, in first sign that it has already wearied of public debate that followed May 12 suicide bombings, orders removal of Jamal A Kashoggi as editor in chief of Al Watan, newspaper that has spoken out against Islamic militancy; move dismays those who believe religious institutions propagate extremist ideas; members of religious establishment feel vindicated; paper, partly owned by prominent descendants of late King Faisal, has been leading voice calling for openness and reform in kingdom; opened unprecedented petition on its Web site asking rulers to curb extremist religious discourse (M)

2003-05-29.title: Hiroshima Journal; Teaching Youth to Start Worrying About the Bomb
2003-05-29.abstract: Japanese survivors of World War II atomic bombings of Hiroshima and Nagasaki fear country is becoming increasingly blase about remembering bombings; Hiroshima's entire image and economy are linked to horrendous final days of war; city officials say visits by Japanese travelers are down, and commissions are formed to reverse trend; one survivor, who regularly tells her story to classrooms full of students, says each year students' stares grow blanker and their questions grow more stilted; photos; map (M)

2003-05-29.title: Bush Visit Will Lift Poland to Status of Special Friend
2003-05-29.abstract: Pres Bush's scheduled stop in Poland is to thank country for supporting American policy in Iraq when bigger countries of NATO opposed it; visit signals that in Washington's eyes, Poland has become important ally, even special friend; it is strange and even improbable role for country whose tragic and tormented history has kept it in shadows for centuries; alongside any awkwardness Poland may feel at being caught between Europe and US goes unaccustomed security and pride at standing on same battlefields as Americans, particularly in Iraq, where Poland is to send 1,500 soldiers to keep peace and will command one of four occupation zones; photo (M)

2003-05-29.title: Saudis Arrest 8 in Deadly Riyadh Bombing
2003-05-29.abstract: Saudi Interior Min Prince Nayef announces capture of eight suspected militants wanted in connection with bombing attacks on residential compounds in Riyadh; says police raids around holy city of Medina led to total of 11 arrests, including foreign-born wives of three of suspects; American Amb Robert W Jordan says United States believes chances are high that another attack will take place despite arrests; Prince Nayef says those arrested include three radical clerics known for their support of militant causes; Saudi and American officials say Al Qaeda remains prime suspect (M)

2003-05-29.title: Quarantine Hits Toronto School In SARS Fight
2003-05-29.abstract: Health authorities in Canada quarantine as many as 2,000 students, teachers and staff members of large parochial school on outskirts of Toronto that was closed after 11th-grade student attended classes for two days last week while showing symptoms of SARS; new quarantine underscores potential seriousness of new outbreak of SARS, only two weeks after local and international authorities declared it was no longer spreading in Toronto; second potential new case of SARS is being investigated in small Ontario community of Parry Sound after resident there develops SARS-type symptoms following visit to affected hospital in Tornoto; 70 residents of Parry Sound are in quarantine; possibility that disease has affected fifth hospital in metropolitan Toronto area is also under investigation after new wave of illness is reported; two elderly SARS patients die, bringing total to 29 deaths in Toronto area in three months; photo (M)

2003-05-29.title: Kurds Celebrate Election of Mayor in Kirkuk
2003-05-29.abstract: Kurds in Kirkuk, city long dominated by Arabs from Baath Party of Saddam Hussein, celebrate election of Abdul Rahman Mustafa as mayor; vote for mayor follows city council elections that give Kurds largest block of votes on 30-seat council; American-organized elections in Kirkuk are significant because city is center of Iraq's vast northern oil resources, and because it has been focal point of ethnic tension between Arabs and Kurds; Arab delegates are subdued even though Arab, Ismail Ahmed Rajab, is elected deputy mayor; photos (M)

2003-05-30.title: Criticism Takes Toll on South Korean Leader
2003-05-30.abstract: Pres Roh Moo Hyun of South Korea is fighting for his political life barely three months into his five-year term; faces daunting list of woes, including corruption investigations involving some longtime associates and his older brother; calls news conference to fend off accusations of corruption, but news media coverage is strongly skeptical, saying there are still many unanswered questions; his recent visit to Washington, during which he met with Pres Bush and strongly reaffirmed country's attachment to its 50-year alliance with US, was supposed to be victory for him; it turned into political debacle at home, after leftist student groups and others accused him of caving in to Washington after campaigning on theme of greater independence; photo (M)

2003-05-30.title: Number of SARS Cases Falls Sharply in China
2003-05-30.abstract: China has nearly tamed the SARS epidemic, with new cases dwindling and no evidence of the wildfire spread in the hinterlands that experts had feared.

2003-05-30.title: Britain to Grant Iranian Dissident Refugee Status
2003-05-30.abstract: British government says it will grant refugee status to Abas Amini, Iranian poet and political dissident who sewed up his lips, eyelids and ears to protest what he says are injustices in way Britain treats asylum seekers; Amini, who will be allowed to remain in Britain, vows to leave stitches intact and to continue his hunger strike on behalf of other asylum seekers (S)

2003-05-30.title: 50 Years Later, a Lone Man Stands at Top of the World
2003-05-30.abstract: Sergei Larin, lone Russian climber, reaches summit of Mount Everest on 50th anniversary of first ascent by Sir Edmund Hillary and Tenzing Norgay; gale winds at base camp prevent several American expeditions from trying to reach summit to mark anniversary; they may not have another chance, because climbing season closes on June 1; Hillary, who favors restricting number of climbers on mountain, has inveighed against softening of Everest ethic; says many would-be climbers use base camp as place to drink; several veteran climbers also lament 'commercialism' and 'circus' atmosphere of Everest; photo; map (M)

2003-05-30.title: World Briefing | Europe: Italy: Berlusconi Immunity Plan
2003-05-30.abstract: Italian Parliament is expected to pass bill submitted by supporters of Prime Minister Silvio Berlusconi that protects those serving in top five political posts from facing trial while in office; opposition leaders claim bill is written to allow Berlusconi, currently involved in corruption trial, to evade prosecution (S)

2003-05-31.title: ARREST IN PEARL CASE
2003-05-31.abstract: Pakistani officials report arrest of Qari Abdul Hayee, Islamic militant believed to have helped plan and carry out kidnapping and murder of American journalist Daniel Pearl; Hayee is member of banned sectarian group Lashkar-e-Jhangvi and among most wanted militants in Pakistan (S)

2003-05-31.title: Psychiatrists Rebuke China For Blocking Inspection Visit
2003-05-31.abstract: World Psychiatric Assn calls on China to fulfill its promise to let international experts examine charges that psychiatry has been misused; China has agreed to cooperate, but ground rules for visit have not been determined; China's psychatric practices have been criticized in recent years since hundreds of members of Falun Gong movement were declared delusional and were forcibly hospitalized (M)

2003-05-31.title: Blair Denies Britain Distorted Reports on Iraqi Weapons
2003-05-31.abstract: British Prime Min Tony Blair angrily denies charges that in making case for war in Iraq, his government exaggerated intelligence reports showing that Saddam Hussein possessed weapons of mass destruction; refutes BBC, which said report asserting that Hussein had capacity to deploy chemical and biological weapons within 45 minutes had been made 'sexier' on Blair's orders and against judgment of intelligence service (M)

2003-05-31.title: Europe Awaits, With Bated Breath
2003-05-31.abstract: Pres Bush arrives in Europe, where there is both hope that trans-Atlantic rift can be healed and skepticism about America's desire to rebuild Europe-American partnership; Bush's words will be dissected to see if administration intends to foster European unity or divide it into 'old' and 'new' Europe; some European newspapers are treating failure to find weapons of mass destruction in Iraq as blow to Bush's credibility that will harm prospects for future cooperation between Europe and US (M)

2003-05-31.title: In Monaco, glamour in the fast lane
2003-05-31.abstract: MONACO:—  Take a fabulous and unique diamond. Add a royal family, a black-tie ball, fashion models and movie stars, Ferraris, Jaguars and spacious yachts. Throw in an exclusive three-night party with star disk jockeys, dancers and circus acts.
